# Coding Brain

Coding Brain is a local TUI for supervising Codex through judgment and learning.

Current product boundary:
- Live shows normalized activity and attention for active sessions.
- Review collects denials, corrections, and uncertain decisions worth teaching from.
- Scorecard reports decision quality and learning progress.
- Lifecycle and permission hooks record bounded activity before transcript discovery catches up.
- Session switching uses supported terminal APIs or optional Agent Deck navigation.
- Coding Brain does not provide a mailbox, scheduler, durable task queue, dependency engine, or distributed coordinator.

Current paths:
- User config: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/coding-brain/config.toml
- User state: $XDG_STATE_HOME/coding-brain/
- Project config: .coding-brain.toml
- Stable project identity: .coding-brain/project.toml

Install and activate:
- cargo install coding-brain
- coding-brain init
- coding-brain doctor
- restart Codex and inspect /hooks
- coding-brain

The crates.io package, executable, and internal Rust crates use the coding-brain namespace. There is no automatic migration or compatibility executable. Confirmed purge is irreversible; old global data remains available for rollback until purge.

Documentation:
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/quickstart/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/configuration/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/reference/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/terminal-support/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/troubleshooting/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/contributing/
